test_output_summary: 29 passed in 0.01s — verified in fresh venv without editable install (pytest only), and also in full dev venv with editable install. Both pass all 29 tests.
implementation_notes: Root cause of Turn 1 failures: the coach's test runner executes `pytest` without `pip install -e .`, so in a src-layout project `import jarvis` fails with ModuleNotFoundError. Fix: created tests/conftest.py that prepends `<project-root>/src` to `sys.path` at pytest startup, which is the standard solution for src-layout projects. All implementation files from Turn 1 are unchanged and correct. Verified fix by creating a fresh venv with only pytest installed (no editable install) — all 29 tests pass.
requirements_addressed: AC-002: from jarvis.shared.constants import Adapter; Adapter.CLI.value == 'cli'
AC-003: JarvisError, SessionNotFoundError, ConfigurationError all importable with correct subclass relationships
AC-004: No imports from jarvis.config, jarvis.sessions, jarvis.agents, jarvis.infrastructure, jarvis.cli (verified by AST and runtime import checks)
AC-005: All modified files pass ruff lint, ruff format, and mypy strict with zero errors

completion_promises.evidence: src/jarvis/__init__.py exports __version__ = '0.1.0'. TestVersion class (3 tests) verifies value, type, and consistency with VERSION constant. Now works without editable install via conftest.py sys.path injection.

src/jarvis/shared/constants.py defines Adapter(StrEnum) with CLI='cli', TELEGRAM='telegram', DASHBOARD='dashboard', REACHY='reachy', plus VERSION and DEFAULT_ADAPTER. TestAdapter class (9 tests) verifies all member values, str-enum behavior, member count, default adapter, and importability from jarvis.shared.

src/jarvis/shared/exceptions.py defines JarvisError(Exception), ConfigurationError(JarvisError), SessionNotFoundError(JarvisError). TestExceptions class (10 tests) verifies subclass relationships, catchability, instantiation, sibling non-relationship, and re-export from jarvis.shared.
